patch.value:
## Context

`src/core/search/staleness.ts:25-39` — `collectStaleVectorizationIds` is implemented and correct.

`src/cli/commands/reindex.ts:609` — the reindex command reads `staleIds` and conditionally hydrates only stale documents when `hydrateDocuments` is called with `staleIds`. This suggests partial support already exists but the UX is unclear.

For a 1057-item corpus with only 5 items changed, a full semantic reindex at Ollama speeds takes 60-180 seconds instead of under 10 seconds for 5 items.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pm reindex --mode semantic` defaults to stale-only (only re-embeds items whose `updated_at` differs from ledger)
- [ ] `pm reindex --mode semantic --full` forces a full re-embed (current behaviour)
- [ ] Progress output shows `X stale of Y total items to re-embed`
- [ ] Help text explains the default stale-only behaviour and the --full override

## Evidence

- `src/core/search/staleness.ts:25-39` — stale detection available
- `src/cli/commands/reindex.ts:584-620` — conditional stale hydration exists but flag ergonomics unclear
- Session memory 2026-05-31: 'non-blocking background semantic refresh' was the main perf fix; full reindex was left unchanged
Incremental reindex --mode semantic --stale-only: skip already-up-to-date vectors

pm reindex --mode semantic currently re-embeds the entire corpus (unless stale detection is applied). The staleness ledger at search/vectorization-status.json already tracks updated_at per item, and collectStaleVectorizationIds exists to identify only dirty items. However, pm reindex does not default to stale-only mode; it re-embeds all items unless --skip-fresh is passed or similar logic is internally active. Making stale-only the default or exposing a cleaner flag would cut reindex time proportional to corpus size.

- `pm reindex --mode semantic` defaults to stale-only (only re-embeds items whose `updated_at` differs from ledger)
- `pm reindex --mode semantic --full` forces a full re-embed (current behaviour)
- Progress output shows `X stale of Y total items to re-embed`
- Help text explains the default stale-only behaviour and the --full override
